Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff (Added To Calendar For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
Off Calendar Dec-22-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
(Mini-minutes continued from previous entry)

Fact discovery cut-off date is March 24, 2017. Expert discovery cut-off date is April 7, 2017. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than January 27, 2017. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. No later than January 5, 2017 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than January 5, 2017 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on February 15, 2017 at 2:00p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on April 10, 2017 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by April 6, 2017 at 2:00p.m. There is no opposition to plaintiffs' motion. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Not Reported. =(503/GLW)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Dec-22-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:

Kimberly Chu, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415.898.1555), appearing for Plaintiffs / Moving Parties;

Constance Fraenkel of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel;

Thomas Steig of PRINDLE, GOETZ, BARNES & REINHOLTZ, LLP (415-788-8354), appearing for Defendant SYD Carpenter, Marine Contractor, Inc.;

Simran Mahal of WALSWORTH FRANKLIN BEVINS & McCALL, LLP (415.781.7072), appearing for Defendant Thomas Dee Engineering.

RULING: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF.

Matter called, hearing held. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). Trial date is April 10, 2017 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is April 21, 2017. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is April 6, 2017. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than February 24, 2017. Responses to the demand are due no later than March 10, 2017. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by April 3, 2017 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on April 6, 2017. (Mini-minutes contined, see next entry)
